CheckTitle: AI Agent Runtime Dependencies Must Be Verified via Checksums or Signatures
CheckType: Software and Configuration Checks/AI Agent Security Best Practices
Description: **AI Agent Runtime Security**. Verifies that all runtime dependencies downloaded by an AI agent are verified against cryptographic checksums or signatures before use. Without integrity verification, a compromised package registry or a man-in-the-middle attacker can substitute a malicious version of a dependency, achieving code execution inside the agent process.
Risk: AI agents typically depend on LLM client libraries, orchestration frameworks (LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen), and utility packages that are regularly updated. Any of these supply chain nodes can be compromised. Without checksum or signature verification at install time, a typosquatted or backdoored package can be silently introduced, giving an attacker persistent code execution and access to all credentials available to the agent.

Code.Other:
1. Use lock files with hashes (pip-tools hashes, poetry.lock with hash verification, npm --require-lock) and verify them at install time.
2. Enable PyPI hash checking: pip install --require-hashes -r requirements.txt.
3. Sign container images and agent artifacts with Sigstore/Cosign and verify signatures in the CI/CD pipeline and at admission.
4. Use in-toto attestations or SLSA provenance to document the full build pipeline for each dependency.
5. Mirror approved packages in a private registry with mandatory checksum scanning on ingest.

Recommendation.Text: Use lock files with cryptographic hashes for all dependencies and verify them at install time. Sign and verify container images with Sigstore/Cosign, and use a private package mirror with mandatory integrity scanning.

Notes: ASPM-066 check from the Prowler ASPM check suite.
